problems.title: Two Sum
problems.prompt: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
problems.solutionExplanation: One-pass hash map: for each number, check if its complement (target - n) is already in the map; if so, return those indices. Otherwise store the current number and its index. Time O(n), space O(n).

problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.prompt: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. An input string is valid if open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets and in the correct order.
problems.solutionExplanation: Use a stack: push opening brackets, and on a closing bracket pop and check it matches the corresponding opener. If the stack is empty at the end, the string is valid. Time O(n), space O(n).

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays
problems.prompt: You are given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, sorted in non-decreasing order. Merge nums2 into nums1 as one sorted array. The final sorted array should be stored inside nums1.
problems.solutionExplanation: Two pointers from the end: compare the current elements of nums1 and nums2 and write the larger one at the back of nums1. This avoids overwriting unmerged values. Time O(m+n), space O(1).

problems.title: Maximum Subarray
problems.prompt: Given an integer array nums, find the subarray with the largest sum, and return its sum.
problems.solutionExplanation: Kadane's algorithm: at each step, either start a new subarray (nums[i]) or extend the current one (cur + nums[i]). Track the best sum seen. Time O(n), space O(1).

problems.title: Reverse Linked List
problems.prompt: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
constraints: The number of nodes is in range [0, 5000]
problems.solution:
def reverse_list(head):
    prev = None
    while head:
        nxt = head.next
        head.next = prev
        prev = head
        head = nxt
    return prev
problems.solutionExplanation: Iterative: maintain a prev node; for each node, save next, point current to prev, then advance prev and current. Return the last node (the new head). Time O(n), space O(1).
